prose:
Method / provenance summary
• Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰), dual descriptor, and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites in furan; headline value reported here is the maximum electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ (atom 5, C).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (RI) in PySCF.
• Geometry provenance: fixed Cartesian coordinates read from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point calculations on the supplied structure for the N, N+1, and N–1 charge states: DFT//input-geometry).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Electronic state: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states for N ± 1 steps generated automatically by the skill.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend, version as in chemkit runtime).
• Convergence & warnings: calculation completed with integrity.status = “ok”; SCFs converged. Engine warnings (verbatim) reproduced below.  
  – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
  – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
  – common header present
• Live log: the PySCF/chemkit run was streamed to a .out file in the working directory (path given at job launch in the session transcript); JSON results saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpep8qlkpq/live.json.

Key numerical results (Mulliken condensed):
Atom  Label  Symbol   f⁺          f⁻        f⁰        Δf (dual descriptor)
  1     1      O    –1.3316182  0.08382155 –0.62389834 –1.4154398
  2     2      C     1.2286866  0.089103093  0.65889484  1.1395835
  3     3      C     1.2270264  0.089304821  0.65816562  1.1377216
  4     4      C     1.9233053  0.1615270  1.0424161  1.7617783
  5     5      C     1.9242931  0.1616371  1.0429651  1.7626560   ← most electrophilic (max f⁺)
  6     6      H    –1.2190639  0.10248412 –0.55828991 –1.3215481
  7     7      H    –1.2191055  0.1025135 –0.55829603 –1.3216190
  8     8      H    –0.76678308  0.10481615 –0.33098346 –0.87159923
  9     9      H    –0.76674059  0.10479266 –0.33097397 –0.87153325

The highest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) is 1.9242931 on atom 5 (a ring carbon), indicating this is the most favorable site for nucleophilic attack. This result is fully computational and follows the specified DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP gas-phase protocol.
